page: OVHcloud c2-120 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) running at 3.0 GHz. The system is configured with 120.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a 400 GB SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the c2-120 demonstrates solid multi-threaded capabilities, highlighted by a strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 253.18 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% tier. Other CPU and memory benchmarks generally align with average performance tiers, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 11504.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 40620.12. Memory bandwidth tests show 113.21 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 38.36 GB/sec for large-block write operations. However, the instance exhibits limitations in LLM text generation, recording a weak 6.29 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model and a poor 37.21 tokens/sec for a small 135M model, reflecting its lack of hardware GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and lack of hyperthreading ensure predictable compute performance without resource contention, making it a stable choice for CPU-bound workloads. The 1:3.75 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and 400 GB local SSD storage suit the instance for multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and software compilation. It is well-suited for workloads such as parallel decompression tasks, medium-scale database operations, and general-purpose compute tasks that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: OVHcloud c2-120 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel Haswell architecture running at 3.0 GHz. Equipped with 120.0 GB of RAM and a 400 GB local SSD, this KVM-based instance delivers reliable, non-hyperthreaded compute performance. Network throughput is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the instance achieves strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance at 253.18 GB/sec, while maintaining average scores in Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Due to the lack of GPU acceleration, LLM text generation speeds are weak, making it unsuitable for AI inference. The c2-120 is highly suitable for multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, static web serving, and dedicated compute workloads requiring predictable CPU performance without resource contention.
og_description: OVHcloud c2-120 is a compute-optimized KVM instance featuring 32 dedicated Intel cores, 120 GB RAM, and a 400 GB local SSD, delivering strong multi-threaded decompression and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
meta_description: OVHcloud c2-120 is a compute-optimized KVM instance featuring 32 dedicated Intel cores, 120 GB of RAM, a 400 GB local SSD, and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated thirty-two core compute-optimized instance featuring local SSD storage, high-speed network bandwidth, and strong multi-threaded processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated physical Intel cores with no hyperthreading for predictable compute performance
120 GB of RAM and 400 GB of local SSD storage
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one complimentary public IPv4 address
Strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance at 253.18 GB/sec
Best suited for multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and static web serving
